This test checks that hover documentation renders doc links using the file and
package context of the declaration.

-- settings.json --
{
	"hoverKind": "FullDocumentation"
}

-- go.mod --
module example.com/doclinks

go 1.20

-- p.go --
// Package p links to [Bar] and [json.Marshal].
package p //@hover(re"package (p)", re"package (p)", packageP)

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	fmt2 "fmt"
)

type Foo struct {
	// Field stores a value.
	Field string
}

// Method prints f.
func (Foo) Method() {}

// Bar uses [json.Marshal], [fmt2.Println], [Bar], [Foo.Method], and [Foo.Field].
func Bar() { //@hover("Bar", "Bar", Bar)
	data, _ := json.Marshal(Foo{})
	fmt2.Println(string(data))
}

func _() {
	_ = bytes.Buffer{}

	// localHandler builds a helper.
	// It mentions [bytes.Buffer] so contextual doc-link rendering is exercised.
	localHandler := func() {}
	_ = localHandler //@hover("localHandler", "localHandler", localHandler)
}

-- @localHandler --
```go
var localHandler func()
```

---

localHandler builds a helper. It mentions [bytes.Buffer](https://pkg.go.dev/bytes#Buffer) so contextual doc-link rendering is exercised.
-- @Bar --
```go
func Bar()
```

---

Bar uses [json.Marshal](https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/json#Marshal), [fmt2.Println](https://pkg.go.dev/fmt#Println), [Bar](https://pkg.go.dev/example.com/doclinks#Bar), [Foo.Method](https://pkg.go.dev/example.com/doclinks#Foo.Method), and [Foo.Field](https://pkg.go.dev/example.com/doclinks#Foo.Field).


---

[`p.Bar` on pkg.go.dev](https://pkg.go.dev/example.com/doclinks#Bar)
-- @packageP --
```go
package p
```

---

Package p links to [Bar](https://pkg.go.dev/example.com/doclinks#Bar) and [json.Marshal](https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/json#Marshal).


---

 - Package path: example.com/doclinks
 - Module: example.com/doclinks
 - Language version: go1.20
